reviews.review_msg: James Acken is enthusiastic, fun, accessible, and very knowledgeable. He's entertaining and humorous as well. He's very lively and "in-synch" with every-day student interests. What more can one ask for?
Great teacher. A little disorganized though.

Prof. Acken is clearly passionate about Celtic history. Hell, he even wore the whole Scottish outfit, kilt and all, to class one day. However, the problem is that he is asked to teach barbarian history and not solely on Celtic history. He focused heavily on the Celts and barely touched on the other barbarian tribes. What a rip off!

Brilliant man, it's sad that others can't see that. No, he does not have a power point display down with what you need for the exam, but he forces the students to think for themselves and engage the text.

Everyone here is actually right. With TAs to keep him straight he was amazing, alone he was befuddled. I think if he had just chucked the syllabus we wouldn't have worried about it and people could've had a better time. At the end of the day, he made Med. Studies interesting to a modernist like me and that's amazing. Totally owned his material.

While not the most organized professor, James is one of few teachers who can make a 4 hour lecture fly by. He does tangentalize, but with an eye to explaining the period to a class which frequently was unwilling to commit to the readings. He is very helpful, but expects students to write their own topics - required in every humanities class. Amazin
AN AMAZING PROFESSOR, BRILLIANT IN EVERY WAY..WITH A COMPLETE WORLD VIEW, GIVEN THE GIFT OF THE GAB, AND ABLE TO RELAY INFO CLEARLY
Pretty horrible -- very unclear, tedious, and an overemotional ticking time bomb. What a waste of $500.

Very frustrating instructor. Gives patronizing lectures about the importance of handing work in on time  but NEVER HANDS ANY WORK BACK! Has an obvious bias toward certain students. Expects every student to have a deep passion for the class content, gives zero direction for papers.Would never take a course with him again.
No Comments

We only covered half of what he set out to do in lectures. He stated that he wanted to avoid 'lecturing' about the text books at all cost, but it seems he wanted to avoid lecturing the entire 2nd half of the course.
What a waste of time. This man loves to brag about how much he knows and couldn't care less about whether anyone else understands him. Dont take anything with him if you value your G.P.A.

Very passionate about his subjects & feels that you must be too. Shows bias in treatment of students esp to those not as passionate as himself. Extensions not granted- he feels that if you don't hand it in on time that your stress level in writing it will speak for itself. Not very compassionate or understanding. Would never take his class again.

Charismatic, passionate, and interesting. However. He spends much of class going off on tangents that do NOT help one understand these difficult texts. His lectures lack context, structure, and, sometimes, clarity. If he tried harder at these things rather than just getting worked up about stuff he would be a fabulous teacher.

I really, really enjoyed James Acken. Wildly passionate and very knowledgeable; however, he enjoys expelling information more than dealing with bureaucratic issues (marking, etc.) and if you do need that structure I wouldn't recommend him. If you're interested in Norse/Celtic lit and lore you won't find a better prof on campus. And yes, he's hot :)

This was the most horrible class I have taken as a student with an extended minor in English. Prof is a poor lecture who enjoys the sound of his own voice. I would not recommend him, nor ever put myself through the misery of taking another class with him. Four hour lectures are spent watching him rant while striking a desk to emphasize his points.

James is amazing.  I've never met someone who is so passionate about thier teaching, and subject area.  He has so much energy; no class is ever boring, and he give you so much information.  He is also one of the most approachable and helpful profs.  His door is always open.  He is also ohhh so hot!!!

He is just so good looking. Wow. That is the only reason I am in his class, but he is nice and because he is good looking everything sounds so much better and makes so much more sense. I would so recommend him.
I LOVE this guys. I've never seen a professor so passionate about his subject matter and I've never been able to be engaged throughout an entire four hour lecture. Awesome.
He is so passionate about what he is teaching that it makes you interested...he is truly a great lecturer
No Comments

engaging, charismatic, and why not, kinda cute.  however, all the personality in the world cannot make this man lecture clearly and effectively.  there is a lot of historical and textual context to take in, organization is key.  power points full of pictures does not help.  hurrah for james, blah for professor acken.

James is extremely into the medieval times. His hair is even cut like a British fop's.  He's very enthusiastic in his teachings which is great, but tends to go seemingly o/t sometimes. A bit of a tough grader, but his assignments are easy. You NEED to read to follow his class, and especially to defend your thesis or comments in tutorial. Great guy!

A very dynamic professor, easily captures your attention, never a dull moment in class.  Has a very wide range of knowledge which he uses effectively to help you understand the material as well as the time period from which it comes.  Fascinating, highly recommend ANY class taught by him.
